H2 formation rate on grain surfaces\n3. Grain charge distribution (for electron recombination on grains)\n4. Simple grain evolution (shattering, coagulation)\n\nThe grain size distribution is discretized into bins spanning\nfrom PAH-sized (3.5 \u00c5) to large grains (1 \u03bcm).\n\"\"\"\n\nimport numpy as np\nfrom ..utils.constants import k_boltz, m_H, eV_to_erg, e_charge\n\n\nclass GrainSizeDistribution:\n    \"\"\"\n    Discretized grain size distribution.\n\n    Uses the MRN (Mathis-Rumpl-Nordsieck 1977) distribution as\n    default: dn/da \u221d a^(-3.5) for a_min < a < a_max.\n\n    Optionally includes a PAH component following\n    Weingartner & Draine (2001).\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    n_bins : int\n        Number of size bins\n    a_min : float\n        Minimum grain size [cm]\n    a_max : float\n        Maximum grain size [cm]\n    composition : str\n        'silicate', 'carbonaceous', or 'mixed'\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, n_bins=20, a_min=3.5e-8, a_max=2.5e-5,\n                 composition='mixed'):\n        self.n_bins = n_bins\n        self.a_min = a_min\n        self.a_max = a_max\n        self.composition = composition\n\n        # Log-spaced bin edges\n        self.a_edges = np.logspace(np.log10(a_min), np.log10(a_max), n_bins + 1)\n        self.a_centers = np.sqrt(self.a_edges[:-1] * self.a_edges[1:])\n        self.da = self.a_edges[1:] - self.a_edges[:-1]\n\n        # Initialize MRN distribution\n        self.dn_da = self._mrn_distribution()\n\n        # Grain material properties\n        if composition == 'silicate':\n            self.rho_grain = 3.5  # g/cm\u00b3\n        elif composition == 'carbonaceous':\n            self.rho_grain = 2.24  # g/cm\u00b3\n        else:\n            self.rho_grain = 3.0  # mixed\n\n        # PAH fraction (fraction of C in PAHs)\n        self.q_PAH = 0.046  # WD01 value for Milky Way\n\n    def _mrn_distribution(self):\n        \"\"\"Standard MRN power-law distribution.\"\"\"\n        # dn/da = C * n_H * a^(-3.5)\n        # Normalization from dust-to-gas ratio\n        # For standard ISM: integral gives tau_V / N_H ~ 5.3e-22\n        C_mrn = 1e-25.13  # normalization constant [cm^(2.5) per H]\n        return C_mrn * self.a_centers**(-3.5)\n\n    def total_cross_section(self, per_H=True):\n        \"\"\"\n        Total geometric cross section of grains.\n\n        Parameters\n        ----------\n        per_H : bool\n",

159\t            # Grain charging parameter for this size\n   160\t            # tau = a * k_B * T / e^2 (reduced temperature)\n   161\t            tau = a * k_boltz * T / e_charge**2\n   162\t\n   163\t            # Mean charge\n   164\t            # Simplified: Z ~ G0 * a / (n_e * sqrt(T))\n   165\t            psi_i = G0 * np.sqrt(T) / n_e * (a / 1e-5)\n   166\t            Z_mean = psi_i / (1.0 + psi_i / 3.0)  # Very approximate\n   167\t\n   168\t            # PE yield for this grain\n   169\t            # Y(a, Z) from WD01 - depends on grain size and charge\n   170\t            # Simplified: Y ~ 0.1 for neutral small grains, decreasing with charge\n   171\t            Y_pe = 0.1 * np.exp(-Z_mean / 3.0) * min(1.0, a / 5e-8)\n   172\t\n   173\t            # UV absorption cross section\n   174\t            Q_abs_UV = min(1.0, 2.0 * np.pi * a / 1e-5)  # efficiency factor\n   175\t            sigma_abs = np.pi * a**2 * Q_abs_UV\n   176\t\n   177\t            # FUV photon rate hitting this grain\n   178\t            F_UV = 1.6e-3 * G0  # erg/cm\u00b2/s\n   179\t            E_photon = 10.0 * eV_to_erg  # mean FUV photon energy ~10 eV\n   180\t            phi_UV = F_UV / E_photon  # photons/cm\u00b2/s\n   181\t\n   182\t            # Mean PE electron energy\n   183\t            # E_pe ~ h*nu - W - Z*e^2/a\n   184\t            # W ~ 4-8 eV (work function)\n   185\t            W = 6.0 * eV_to_erg  # Work function\n   186\t            E_pe = max(E_photon - W - Z_mean * e_charge**2 / max(a, 1e-8), 0)\n   187\t\n   188\t            # Heating rate from this grain size\n   189\t            Gamma_i = n_grain_i * sigma_abs * phi_UV * Y_pe * E_pe\n   190\t\n   191\t            Gamma_total += Gamma_i\n   192\t\n   193\t        return Gamma_total\n   194\t\n   195\t    def h2_formation_rate(self, T, T_dust, a_index=None):\n   196\t        \"\"\"\n   197\t        Compute H2 formation rate coefficient on grain surfaces.\n   198\t\n   199\t        Uses the Cazaux & Tielens (2004) model which accounts for\n   200\t        grain temperature, sticking coefficient, and surface mobility.\n[350 lines total]",

\"\"\"\n        sigma = np.sum(np.pi * self.a_centers**2 * self.dn_da * self.da)\n        return sigma\n\n    def total_surface_area(self, per_H=True):\n        \"\"\"Total grain surface area per H nucleus [cm\u00b2/H].\"\"\"\n        return np.sum(4.0 * np.pi * self.a_centers**2 * self.dn_da * self.da)\n\n    def dust_to_gas_ratio(self):\n        \"\"\"Compute the dust-to-gas mass ratio.\"\"\"\n        mass_per_H = np.sum(\n            (4.0/3.0) * np.pi * self.a_centers**3 * self.rho_grain\n            * self.dn_da * self.da\n        )\n        return mass_per_H / m_H\n\n\nclass GrainPhysics:\n    \"\"\"\n    Grain physics calculations for a PDR cell.\n\n    Computes PE heating, H2 formation, and grain charging\n    from the local grain size distribution and conditions.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, grain_dist=None):\n        if grain_dist is None:\n            grain_dist = GrainSizeDistribution()\n        self.grain_dist = grain_dist\n\n    def photoelectric_heating_from_distribution(self, n_H, T, G0, x_e):\n        \"\"\"\n        Compute PE heating from the actual grain size distribution.\n\n        This is more accurate than the Bakes & Tielens (1994) fit\n        because it accounts for the actual grain population rather\n        than an assumed average.\n\n        Uses the Weingartner & Draine (2001) grain charging model.\n\n        Parameters\n        ----------\n        n_H : float\n            H nuclei density [cm\u207b\u00b3]\n        T : float\n            Gas temperature [K]\n        G0 : float\n            FUV field [Habing units]\n        x_e : float\n            Electron fraction\n\n        Returns\n        -------\n        Gamma_PE : float\n            PE heating rate [erg/cm\u00b3/s]\n        \"\"\"\n        if G0 <= 0:\n            return 0.0\n\n        n_e = max(x_e * n_H, 1e-10 * n_H)\n        gd = self.grain_dist\n\n        Gamma_total = 0.0\n\n        for i in range(gd.n_bins):\n            a = gd.a_centers[i]\n            dn_da_i = gd.dn_da[i]\n            da_i = gd.da[i]\n\n            # Number density of grains in this bin\n            n_grain_i = dn_da_i * da_i * n_H\n\n            # Grain charging parameter for this size\n            # tau = a * k_B * T / e^2 (reduced temperature)\n            tau = a * k_boltz * T / e_charge**2\n\n            # Mean charge\n            # Simplified: Z ~ G0 * a / (n_e * sqrt(T))\n            psi_i = G0 * np.sqrt(T) / n_e * (a / 1e-5)\n            Z_mean = psi_i / (1.0 + psi_i / 3.0)  # Very approximate\n\n            # PE yield for this grain\n            # Y(a, Z) from WD01 - depends on grain size and charge\n            # Simplified: Y ~ 0.1 for neutral small grains, decreasing with charge\n            Y_pe = 0.1 * np.exp(-Z_mean / 3.0) * min(1.0, a / 5e-8)\n\n            # UV absorption cross section\n            Q_abs_UV = min(1.0, 2.0 * np.pi * a / 1e-5)  # efficiency factor\n            sigma_abs = np.pi * a**2 * Q_abs_UV\n\n            # FUV photon rate hitting this grain\n            F_UV = 1.6e-3 * G0  # erg/cm\u00b2/s\n            E_photon = 10.0 * eV_to_erg  # mean FUV photon energy ~10 eV\n            phi_UV = F_UV / E_photon  # photons/cm\u00b2/s\n\n            # Mean PE electron energy\n            # E_pe ~ h*nu - W - Z*e^2/a\n            # W ~ 4-8 eV (work function)\n            W = 6.0 * eV_to_erg  # Work function\n            E_pe = max(E_photon - W - Z_mean * e_charge**2 / max(a, 1e-8), 0)\n\n            # Heating rate from this grain size\n            Gamma_i = n_grain_i * sigma_abs * phi_UV * Y_pe * E_pe\n\n            Gamma_total += Gamma_i\n\n        return Gamma_total\n\n    def h2_formation_rate(self, T, T_dust, a_index=None):\n        \"\"\"\n        Compute H2 formation rate coefficient on grain surfaces.\n\n        Uses the Cazaux & Tielens (2004) model which accounts for\n        grain temperature, sticking coefficient, and surface mobility.\n",

      "code": "        grain temperature, sticking coefficient, and surface mobility.\n\n        Parameters\n        ----------\n        T : float\n            Gas temperature [K]\n        T_dust : float\n            Dust temperature [K]\n        a_index : int, optional\n            If given, compute for a specific grain size bin.\n            Otherwise, sum over all bins.\n\n        Returns\n        -------\n        R_H2 : float\n            H2 formation rate coefficient [cm\u00b3/s]\n            (rate = R_H2 * n(H) * n_H)\n        \"\"\"\n        gd = self.grain_dist\n\n        R_total = 0.0\n\n        # Thermal speed of H atoms\n        v_H = np.sqrt(8.0 * k_boltz * T / (np.pi * m_H))\n\n        # Sticking coefficient (Hollenbach & McKee 1979)\n        S_H = 1.0 / (1.0 + 0.04 * np.sqrt(T + T_dust)\n                     + 0.002 * T + 8e-6 * T**2)\n\n        if a_index is not None:\n            bins = [a_index]\n        else:\n            bins = range(gd.n_bins)\n\n        for i in bins:\n            a = gd.a_centers[i]\n            dn_da_i = gd.dn_da[i]\n            da_i = gd.da[i]\n\n            # Cross section\n            sigma = np.pi * a**2\n\n            # Recombination efficiency on surface\n            # Depends on T_dust: efficient at low T, drops above ~20 K\n            # Cazaux & Tielens (2004) formula\n            if T_dust < 10:\n                epsilon = 1.0\n            elif T_dust < 20:\n                epsilon = 1.0 - 0.05 * (T_dust - 10)\n            elif T_dust < 100:\n                epsilon = 0.5 * np.exp(-(T_dust - 20) / 30.0)\n            else:\n                # Chemisorption allows H2 formation even at high T_dust\n                epsilon = 0.1 * np.exp(-(T_dust - 100) / 200.0)\n\n            # Contribution from this bin\n            R_i = 0.5 * v_H * S_H * epsilon * sigma * dn_da_i * da_i\n\n            R_total += R_i\n\n        return R_total\n\n    def grain_recombination_rate(self, T, x_e, ion_species='C+'):\n        \"\"\"\n        Compute grain-assisted recombination rate for an ion species.\n\n        Important for C+ recombination in PDR surfaces where\n        grain recombination can exceed radiative recombination.\n\n        Following Weingartner & Draine (2001).\n\n        Parameters\n        ----------\n        T : float\n            Gas temperature [K]\n        x_e : float\n            Electron fraction\n        ion_species : str\n            Ion species name\n\n        Returns\n        -------\n        k_gr : float\n            Grain recombination rate coefficient [cm\u00b3/s]\n        \"\"\"\n        gd = self.grain_dist\n\n        # Simplified WD01 grain recombination\n        # k_gr ~ sum over sizes: pi*a^2 * v_ion * S * f(charge)\n\n        # Ion mass (approximate)\n        ion_masses = {'C+': 12, 'S+': 32, 'Si+': 28, 'Fe+': 56, 'H+': 1}\n        m_ion = ion_masses.get(ion_species, 12) * m_H\n\n        v_ion = np.sqrt(8.0 * k_boltz * T / (np.pi * m_ion))\n\n        k_total = 0.0\n        for i in range(gd.n_bins):\n            a = gd.a_centers[i]\n            sigma = np.pi * a**2\n            dn_da_i = gd.dn_da[i]\n            da_i = gd.da[i]\n\n            # Sticking probability for ions (enhanced by Coulomb focusing\n            # for negatively charged grains)\n            S_ion = min(1.0, 1.0 + e_charge**2 / (a * k_boltz * T))\n\n            k_i = v_ion * S_ion * sigma * dn_da_i * da_i\n            k_total += k_i\n\n        return k_total\n\n    def evolve_distribution(self, n_H, T, v_turb, dt):\n        \"\"\"\n        Evolve the grain size distribution due to shattering and coagulation.\n\n        Simple implementation following Hirashita & Yan (2009):\n        - Shattering by grain-grain collisions in turbulence\n        - Coagulation in dense regions\n\n        Parameters\n        ----------\n        n_H : float\n            H nuclei density [cm\u207b\u00b3]\n        T : float\n            Gas temperature [K]\n        v_turb : float\n            Turbulent velocity [cm/s]\n        dt : float\n            Time step [s]\n        \"\"\"\n        gd = self.grain_dist\n\n        # Shattering: moves mass from large to small grains\n        # Rate \u221d n_grain\u00b2 * v_turb * sigma\n        # Only significant in strong shocks or high turbulence\n\n        # Coagulation: moves mass from small to large grains\n        # Rate \u221d n_grain\u00b2 * v_thermal * sigma * sticking\n        # Only significant at high density (n > 10^5)\n\n        # For now, simple relaxation toward MRN\n        tau_relax = 1e14 / (n_H / 1e3)  # ~3 Myr at n=1e3\n\n        if dt < tau_relax * 0.01:\n            return  # No significant evolution\n\n        mrn = gd._mrn_distribution()\n        f = min(dt / tau_relax, 1.0)\n        gd.dn_da = gd.dn_da * (1.0 - f) + mrn * f\n",

      "file_text": "\"\"\"\nTHEMIS Dust Model for PRISM-3D.\n\nImplements the Heterogeneous dust Evolution Model for Interstellar Solids\n(Jones et al. 2013, 2017; Ysard et al. 2024 \u2014 THEMIS 2.0) adapted for\nself-consistent coupling to PDR gas chemistry and thermal balance.\n\nThree grain populations:\n  1. a-C(:H) nano-particles (a = 0.4\u201320 nm): aromatic/aliphatic carbon\n     \u2192 stochastically heated, produce MIR AIB emission\n     \u2192 dominate photoelectric heating\n     \u2192 evolve via UV photo-processing (aromatization, destruction)\n  2. Large a-C(:H) grains (a = 20 nm \u2013 0.2 \u03bcm): carbonaceous grains\n     \u2192 in thermal equilibrium\n     \u2192 contribute to FUV/visible extinction\n  3. Large a-Sil grains (a = 20 nm \u2013 0.3 \u03bcm): amorphous silicates\n     \u2192 in thermal equilibrium\n     \u2192 dominate FIR/submm emission\n\nKey physics:\n  - Band gap E_g controls aromatic vs aliphatic character\n  - UV processing lowers E_g (aliphatic \u2192 aromatic)\n  - Photo-destruction of nano-particles in strong FUV\n  - Size-dependent optical properties Q_abs(a, \u03bb)\n  - Full PE heating from charge distribution (replaces BT94)\n  - Stochastic heating via temperature probability distribution P(T)\n  - Feedback to gas: PE heating, H\u2082 formation, electron balance, UV opacity\n\nReferences:\n  Jones et al. 2013, A&A 558, A62 \u2014 THEMIS framework\n  Jones et al. 2017, A&A 602, A46 \u2014 Global THEMIS model\n  Compi\u00e8gne et al. 2011, A&A 525, A103 \u2014 DustEM code\n  Elyajouri et al. 2024, A&A 685, A76 \u2014 JWST dust evolution Orion Bar\n  Ysard et al. 2024 \u2014 THEMIS 2.0\n\"\"\"\n\nimport numpy as np\nfrom ..utils.constants import (\n    k_boltz, h_planck, c_light, sigma_SB, eV_to_erg,\n    e_charge, m_H, m_e, pc_cm\n)\n\n\n# ============================================================\n# Physical constants for dust\n# ============================================================\n\n# Carbon atom mass\nm_C = 12.0 * m_H  # g\n\n# Debye specific heat: C_grain \u2248 N_atoms \u00d7 k_B at high T\n# At low T, C \u221d T\u00b3 (Debye model)\nDEBYE_T_CARBON = 863.0  # K, Debye temperature for graphite\nDEBYE_T_SILICATE = 500.0  # K, Debye temperature for silicates\n\n# FUV energy range\nE_FUV_MIN = 6.0 * eV_to_erg   # 6 eV\nE_FUV_MAX = 13.6 * eV_to_erg  # 13.6 eV\nE_FUV_MEAN = 10.0 * eV_to_erg # Mean FUV photon energy\n\n# Standard ISRF FUV flux (Habing field)\nF_HABING = 1.6e-3  # erg cm\u207b\u00b2 s\u207b\u00b9 (6-13.6 eV)\n\n# Dust-to-gas mass ratio (standard ISM)\nDUST_TO_GAS_STANDARD = 0.01\n\n# THEMIS band gap for diffuse ISM carbon nano-grains\nEG_DIFFUSE = 0.1   # eV \u2014 partially aromatic (UV-processed in diffuse ISM)\nEG_DENSE = 2.5     # eV \u2014 highly aliphatic (shielded in dense regions)\nEG_AROMATIC = 0.0   # eV \u2014 fully aromatic (graphitic)\n\n\n# ============================================================\n# THEMIS Grain Population\n# ============================================================\n\nclass THEMISPopulation:\n    \"\"\"\n    A single THEMIS grain population (nano-carbons, large carbons, or silicates).\n    \n    Each population has a size distribution, material properties, and\n    optical properties that depend on size and band gap.\n    \n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    name : str\n        Population name ('aC_nano', 'aC_large', 'aSil')\n    a_min : float\n        Minimum grain radius [cm]\n    a_max : float\n        Maximum grain radius [cm]\n    n_bins : int\n        Number of size bins\n    rho : float\n        Material density [g/cm\u00b3]\n    E_g : float\n        Band gap [eV] \u2014 controls aromatic/aliphatic character\n        Only meaningful for carbonaceous grains.\n    slope : float\n        Power-law slope of dn/da (default -3.5 for MRN)\n    mass_fraction : float\n        Fraction of total dust mass in this population\n    \"\"\"\n    \n    def __init__(self, name, a_min, a_max, n_bins=15,\n                 rho=2.24, E_g=0.1, slope=-3.5, mass_fraction=0.1):\n        self.name = name\n        self.a_min = a_min\n        self.a_max = a_max\n        self.n_bins = n_bins\n        self.rho = rho\n        self.E_g = E_g  # Band gap [eV]\n        self.slope = slope\n        self.mass_fraction = mass_fraction\n        \n        # Size grid\n        self.a_edges = np.logspace(np.log10(a_min), np.log10(a_max), n_bins + 1)\n        self.a = np.sqrt(self.a_edges[:-1] * self.a_edges[1:])  # geometric mean\n        self.da = self.a_edges[1:] - self.a_edges[:-1]\n        \n        # Size distribution dn/da per H nucleus [cm\u207b\u00b9 per H]\n        raw = self.a**slope\n        # Normalize to target mass fraction\n        mass_integral = np.sum((4./3.) * np.pi * self.a**3 * rho * raw * self.da)\n        target_mass = mass_fraction * DUST_TO_GAS_STANDARD * m_H\n        self.dn_da = raw * (target_mass / mass_integral) if mass_integral > 0 else raw * 0\n        \n        # Number of carbon atoms per grain (for nano-carbons)\n        self.N_C = (4./3.) * np.pi * self.a**3 * rho / m_C\n        \n        # Precompute optical properties\n        self._compute_optics()\n    \n    def _compute_optics(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Compute absorption and scattering efficiencies.\n        \n        Uses analytic approximations calibrated to THEMIS/DustEM tables.\n        Q_abs depends on size, wavelength, and band gap.\n        \"\"\"\n        # FUV absorption efficiency (integrated 6-13.6 eV)\n        # Small grains: Q_abs \u2248 1 (geometric limit for a > \u03bb/2\u03c0)\n        # Very small grains: Q_abs \u221d a (Rayleigh regime)\n        lambda_FUV = 0.1e-4  # ~1000 \u00c5 = 0.1 \u03bcm representative FUV wavelength\n        x = 2 * np.pi * self.a / lambda_FUV  # size parameter\n        \n        # Carbonaceous grains (higher FUV absorption than silicates)\n        if 'aC' in self.name:\n            self.Q_abs_FUV = np.where(x > 1, 1.0, x)\n            # Band gap effect: lower E_g \u2192 more UV absorption\n            E_g_factor = 1.0 + 0.5 * (0.1 - min(self.E_g, 0.5))\n            self.Q_abs_FUV *= np.clip(E_g_factor, 0.5, 1.5)\n        else:\n            # Silicates: lower FUV efficiency\n            self.Q_abs_FUV = np.where(x > 1, 0.7, 0.7 * x)\n        \n        # Optical/NIR absorption (for extinction curve)\n        lambda_V = 0.55e-4  # V band\n        x_V = 2 * np.pi * self.a / lambda_V\n        self.Q_abs_V = np.where(x_V > 1, 1.0, x_V**2)\n        \n        # FIR/submm emissivity: Q_abs \u221d a * (\u03bd/\u03bd\u2080)^\u03b2\n        # \u03b2 \u2248 2 for silicates, 1.5-2 for amorphous carbon\n        self.beta_FIR = 2.0 if 'Sil' in self.name else 1.8\n        \n        # Reference: Q_abs at 250 \u03bcm for a = 0.1 \u03bcm grain\n        self.Q_abs_250_ref = 1e-3 if 'Sil' in self.name else 5e-4\n    \n    def Q_abs_lambda(self, wavelength_cm):\n        \"\"\"\n        Absorption efficiency at a given wavelength for all size bins.\n        \n        Parameters\n        ----------\n        wavelength_cm : float\n            Wavelength [cm]\n        \n        Returns\n        -------\n        Q_abs : ndarray, shape (n_bins,)\n        \"\"\"\n        x = 2 * np.pi * self.a / wavelength_cm\n        \n        if wavelength_cm < 0.3e-4:  # UV\n            return self.Q_abs_FUV\n        elif wavelength_cm < 5e-4:  # Optical/NIR\n            return np.where(x > 1, 1.0, x**2)\n        else:  # FIR/submm\n            nu = c_light / wavelength_cm\n            nu_ref = c_light / 250e-4  # 250 \u03bcm reference\n            Q_ref = self.Q_abs_250_ref * (self.a / 1e-5)  # scales with a\n            return Q_ref * (nu / nu_ref)**self.beta_FIR\n    \n    def cross_section_FUV(self):\n        \"\"\"Total FUV extinction cross section per H [cm\u00b2/H].\"\"\"\n        return np.sum(np.pi * self.a**2 * self.Q_abs_FUV * self.dn_da * self.da)\n    \n    def total_surface_area(self):\n        \"\"\"Total grain surface area per H [cm\u00b2/H].\"\"\"\n        return np.sum(4.0 * np.pi * self.a**2 * self.dn_da * self.da)\n    \n    def total_number(self):\n        \"\"\"Total grain number per H.\"\"\"\n        return np.sum(self.dn_da * self.da)\n    \n    def mass_per_H(self):\n        \"\"\"Total grain mass per H nucleus [g/H].\"\"\"\n        return np.sum((4./3.) * np.pi * self.a**3 * self.rho * self.dn_da * self.da)\n\n\n# ============================================================\n# Full THEMIS Dust Model\n# ============================================================\n\nclass THEMISDust:\n    \"\"\"\n    Complete THEMIS dust model with three populations.\n    \n    Provides:\n    - Photoelectric heating from grain charge distribution\n    - H\u2082 formation on grain surfaces\n    - Dust temperature (equilibrium for large, stochastic for nano)\n    - Dust SED per cell\n    - Dust evolution (nano-grain depletion by UV)\n    \n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    f_nano : float\n        Scaling factor for nano-grain abundance relative to diffuse ISM.\n        1.0 = standard, 0.07 = Orion Bar edge (Elyajouri+ 2024)\n    E_g : float\n        Band gap of nano-carbons [eV]. 0.1 = diffuse ISM, higher = denser/shielded\n    \"\"\"\n    \n    def __init__(self, f_nano=1.0, E_g=0.1):\n        self.f_nano = f_nano\n        self.E_g = E_g\n        \n        # Build three populations following THEMIS diffuse ISM model\n        # Mass fractions: ~25% in nano-C, ~8% in large a-C, ~67% in silicates\n        self.nano_C = THEMISPopulation(\n            'aC_nano',\n            a_min=4e-8,    # 0.4 nm\n            a_max=2e-7,    # 20 nm (nano-grain regime)\n            n_bins=12,\n            rho=1.6,       # g/cm\u00b3 for a-C:H (lower than graphite)\n            E_g=E_g,\n            slope=-3.5,\n            mass_fraction=0.25 * f_nano  # Depleted by f_nano\n        )\n        \n        self.large_C = THEMISPopulation(\n            'aC_large',\n            a_min=2e-7,    # 20 nm\n            a_max=2e-5,    # 0.2 \u03bcm\n            n_bins=10,\n            rho=1.6,\n            E_g=0.1,       # Large grains are always UV-processed on surfaces\n            slope=-3.5,\n            mass_fraction=0.08\n        )\n        \n        self.silicate = THEMISPopulation(\n            'aSil',\n            a_min=2e-7,    # 20 nm\n            a_max=3e-5,    # 0.3 \u03bcm\n            n_bins=10,\n            rho=3.5,\n            slope=-3.5,\n            mass_fraction=0.67\n        )\n        \n        self.populations = [self.nano_C, self.large_C, self.silicate]\n    \n    # --------------------------------------------------------\n    # Photoelectric heating\n    # --------------------------------------------------------\n    \n    def photoelectric_heating(self, n_H, T, G0, x_e):\n        \"\"\"\n        Compute PE heating from the full grain charge distribution.\n        \n        This replaces the Bakes & Tielens (1994) analytic fit with a\n        proper WD01-style calculation summing over all grain sizes.\n        The key improvement: accounts for nano-grain depletion and\n        band-gap-dependent yields.\n        \n        Parameters\n        ----------\n        n_H : float\n            H number density [cm\u207b\u00b3]\n        T : float\n            Gas temperature [K]\n        G0 : float\n            FUV field [Habing units]\n        x_e : float\n            Electron fraction\n        \n        Returns\n        -------\n        Gamma_PE : float\n            PE heating rate [erg cm\u207b\u00b3 s\u207b\u00b9]\n        \"\"\"\n        if G0 <= 0 or T <= 0:\n            return 0.0\n        \n        n_e = max(x_e, 1e-10) * n_H\n        Gamma_total = 0.0\n        \n        for pop in self.populations:\n            for i in range(pop.n_bins):\n                a = pop.a[i]\n                n_gr = pop.dn_da[i] * pop.da[i] * n_H  # grain number density\n                \n                if n_gr <= 0:\n                    continue\n                \n                # Grain charging parameter (WD01 Eq. 2)\n                psi = G0 * np.sqrt(T) / n_e\n                \n                # Mean grain charge Z(a, psi)\n                # Small grains can have Z ~ 0 or negative\n                # Large grains tend to be positively charged\n                N_C_grain = (4./3.) * np.pi * a**3 * pop.rho / m_C\n                \n                # Ionization potential (work function + charging)\n                # a-C(:H): IP depends on band gap\n                if 'aC' in pop.name:\n                    W = (4.0 + pop.E_g) * eV_to_erg  # Work function\n                    Y_0 = 0.14  # Base yield for a-C\n                else:\n                    W = 8.0 * eV_to_erg  # Silicate work function\n                    Y_0 = 0.05  # Lower yield for silicates\n                \n                # Mean charge from WD01 simplified charging balance\n                # Ionization rate \u221d G0 * sigma * Y\n                # Recombination rate \u221d n_e * v_e * sigma * S_e(Z)\n                v_e = np.sqrt(8 * k_boltz * T / (np.pi * m_e))\n                \n                # Solve for Z iteratively (quick: 3 iterations)\n                Z = 0.0\n                for _ in range(3):\n                    # PE yield decreases with positive charge\n                    Y = Y_0 * np.exp(-max(Z, 0) * e_charge**2 / (a * E_FUV_MEAN))\n                    Y = max(Y, 1e-4)\n                    \n                    # Electron sticking (Coulomb enhanced for Z > 0)\n                    S_e = 1.0 if Z <= 0 else 1.0 + Z * e_charge**2 / (a * k_boltz * T)\n                    \n                    # Charge balance: G0 * sigma_abs * phi_UV * Y = n_e * v_e * pi*a\u00b2 * S_e\n                    phi_UV = F_HABING * G0 / E_FUV_MEAN\n                    sigma_abs = np.pi * a**2 * pop.Q_abs_FUV[i]\n                    \n                    R_ion = sigma_abs * phi_UV * Y\n                    R_rec = n_e * v_e * np.pi * a**2 * S_e\n                    \n                    if R_rec > 0:\n                        Z = R_ion / R_rec - 0.5  # Approximate\n                        Z = max(Z, -2.0)  # Can't be very negative\n                \n                # PE electron energy\n                E_pe = max(E_FUV_MEAN - W - Z * e_charge**2 / max(a, 1e-8), 0)\n                \n                # Heating rate from this grain\n                Gamma_i = n_gr * sigma_abs * phi_UV * Y * E_pe\n                Gamma_total += Gamma_i\n        \n        return Gamma_total\n    \n    # --------------------------------------------------------\n    # H\u2082 formation\n    # --------------------------------------------------------\n    \n    def h2_formation_rate(self, T, T_dust):\n        \"\"\"\n        H\u2082 formation rate coefficient on all grain surfaces [cm\u00b3/s].\n        \n        Accounts for:\n        - Size-dependent surface area\n        - Temperature-dependent sticking\n        - Chemisorbed sites on carbonaceous grains (high-T formation)\n        - Physisorbed sites on silicates (low-T formation)\n        \n        Returns R such that formation rate = R \u00d7 n(H) \u00d7 n_H [cm\u207b\u00b3 s\u207b\u00b9]\n        \"\"\"\n        v_H = np.sqrt(8.0 * k_boltz * T / (np.pi * m_H))\n        \n        # Sticking coefficient (Hollenbach & McKee 1979, updated)\n        S_H = 1.0 / (1.0 + 0.04 * np.sqrt(T + T_dust)\n                     + 0.002 * T + 8e-6 * T**2)\n        \n        R_total = 0.0\n        \n        for pop in self.populations:\n            for i in range(pop.n_bins):\n                sigma = np.pi * pop.a[i]**2\n                dn_da_i = pop.dn_da[i]\n                da_i = pop.da[i]\n                \n                # Surface recombination efficiency\n                if 'Sil' in pop.name:\n                    # Silicates: physisorption-dominated\n                    # Efficient at T_dust < 20 K, drops sharply above\n                    if T_dust < 14:\n                        eps = 1.0\n                    elif T_dust < 25:\n                        eps = np.exp(-(T_dust - 14) / 5.0)\n                    else:\n                        eps = 0.01\n                else:\n                    # Carbonaceous: chemisorption allows high-T formation\n                    # Cazaux & Tielens (2004): efficient up to ~400 K\n                    if T_dust < 50:\n                        eps = 0.8\n                    elif T_dust < 400:\n                        eps = 0.8 * np.exp(-(T_dust - 50) / 200.0)\n                    else:\n                        eps = 0.01\n                    # Eley-Rideal mechanism at very high T\n                    # (KOSMA-\u03c4 2022 update)\n                    eps = max(eps, 0.005)\n                \n                R_total += 0.5 * v_H * S_H * eps * sigma * dn_da_i * da_i\n        \n        return R_total\n    \n    # --------------------------------------------------------\n    # Dust temperature\n    # --------------------------------------------------------\n    \n    def equilibrium_temperature(self, G0, A_V=0):\n        \"\"\"\n        Equilibrium dust temperature for large grains.\n        \n        Balances FUV/optical absorption against FIR emission.\n        Returns dict of {population_name: T_dust_array}.\n        \"\"\"\n        T_eq = {}\n        \n        for pop in [self.large_C, self.silicate]:\n            # Absorbed power per grain: sigma_abs * F_UV * exp(-1.8 AV)\n            # + optical/NIR ISRF contribution\n            F_abs = F_HABING * G0 * np.exp(-1.8 * A_V)\n            # Add optical ISRF (roughly equal to FUV in energy)\n            F_abs += F_HABING * 0.5 * np.exp(-A_V)\n            \n            P_abs = np.pi * pop.a**2 * pop.Q_abs_FUV * F_abs\n            \n            # Emitted power: 4\u03c0 * sigma_SB * T^4 * <Q_abs>_Planck * 4\u03c0 a\u00b2\n            # <Q_abs>_Planck ~ Q_0 * (a/a_0) * (T/T_0)^beta\n            # Solving: T = [P_abs / (16\u03c0\u00b2 \u03c3_SB a\u00b2 Q_eff)]^(1/(4+beta))\n            \n            beta = pop.beta_FIR\n            Q_eff_ref = pop.Q_abs_250_ref  # at 250 \u03bcm, a = 0.1 \u03bcm, T ~ 20K\n            # Scale: Q_eff(a,T) \u2248 Q_ref * (a/1e-5) * (T/20)^beta\n            # This gives: P_abs = 16\u03c0\u00b2 \u03c3_SB a\u00b2 Q_ref (a/1e-5) T^(4+beta) / 20^beta\n            \n            denom = 16 * np.pi**2 * sigma_SB * pop.a**2 * Q_eff_ref * (pop.a / 1e-5) / 20.0**beta\n            denom = np.maximum(denom, 1e-50)\n            \n            T = np.power(P_abs / denom, 1.0 / (4.0 + beta))\n            T = np.clip(T, 5.0, 2000.0)  # Physical bounds\n            \n            T_eq[pop.name] = T\n        \n        return T_eq\n    \n    def stochastic_temperature_distribution(self, G0, A_V=0, n_T_bins=50):\n        \"\"\"\n        Temperature probability distribution P(T) for nano-grains.\n        \n        Small grains undergo temperature spikes from individual\n        UV photon absorptions. The time-averaged P(T) determines\n        the MIR emission spectrum (PAH bands, VSG continuum).\n        \n        Uses the method of Draine & Li (2001): solve the thermal\n        balance with discretized temperature bins.\n        \n        Returns\n        -------\n        T_grid : ndarray (n_T_bins,)\n        P_T : ndarray (n_bins, n_T_bins)\n            P(T) for each size bin of nano-carbons\n        \"\"\"\n        T_grid = np.logspace(np.log10(5.0), np.log10(2500.0), n_T_bins)\n        pop = self.nano_C\n        P_T = np.zeros((pop.n_bins, n_T_bins))\n        \n        F_abs = F_HABING * G0 * np.exp(-1.8 * A_V)\n        phi_UV = F_abs / E_FUV_MEAN  # FUV photon flux [cm\u207b\u00b2 s\u207b\u00b9]\n        \n        for i in range(pop.n_bins):\n            a = pop.a[i]\n            N_C = pop.N_C[i]\n            \n            if N_C < 10:\n                N_C = 10  # Minimum for meaningful heat capacity\n            \n            # Photon absorption rate\n            sigma_abs = np.pi * a**2 * pop.Q_abs_FUV[i]\n            R_abs = sigma_abs * phi_UV  # photons/s\n            \n            if R_abs <= 0:\n                P_T[i, 0] = 1.0\n                continue\n            \n            # Heat capacity: C(T) = N_atoms * k_B * f_Debye(T/T_D)\n            # Simplified: C \u2248 N_C * k_B * min(1, (T/T_D)\u00b3) for carbon\n            T_D = DEBYE_T_CARBON\n            C_grain = N_C * k_boltz * np.minimum(1.0, (T_grid / T_D)**3)\n            C_grain = np.maximum(C_grain, k_boltz)  # Floor\n            \n            # Temperature after absorbing one FUV photon\n            # \u0394T \u2248 E_photon / C(T_base)\n            # For small grains: T_peak can be >> T_eq\n            \n            # Cooling rate: dE/dt = -4\u03c0 a\u00b2 \u03c3_SB <Q_abs> T\u2074\n            # \u2248 4\u03c0 a\u00b2 * Q_eff * \u03c3_SB * T^(4+beta)\n            Q_eff = pop.Q_abs_250_ref * (a / 1e-5)\n            cool_rate = 4 * np.pi * a**2 * Q_eff * sigma_SB * T_grid**(4 + pop.beta_FIR) / 20.0**pop.beta_FIR\n            \n            # Cooling time: t_cool = C * T / cool_rate\n            t_cool = C_grain * T_grid / np.maximum(cool_rate, 1e-50)\n            \n            # Time between photon absorptions\n            t_abs = 1.0 / max(R_abs, 1e-30)\n            \n            # Steady-state P(T): balance heating spikes with cooling\n            # Grains spend most time near T_min (just before next photon)\n            # P(T) \u221d t_cool(T) for T near equilibrium,\n            # with spikes extending to T_peak after absorption\n            \n            # Equilibrium temperature (for reference)\n            P_abs_grain = sigma_abs * F_abs\n            denom_eq = 4 * np.pi * a**2 * Q_eff * sigma_SB / 20.0**pop.beta_FIR\n            T_eq_grain = max((P_abs_grain / max(denom_eq, 1e-50))**(1.0/(4+pop.beta_FIR)), 5.0)\n            \n            # For very small grains (N_C < 200): truly stochastic\n            # For larger grains: P(T) \u2192 delta(T - T_eq)\n            if N_C > 500 or t_abs > 100 * t_cool[n_T_bins // 2]:\n                # Thermal equilibrium regime\n                idx_eq = np.argmin(np.abs(T_grid - T_eq_grain))\n                P_T[i, idx_eq] = 1.0\n            else:\n                # Stochastic regime\n                # T_peak after one photon absorption from T_base ~ 5-10 K\n                C_base = N_C * k_boltz * min(1.0, (10.0 / T_D)**3)\n                T_peak = min((E_FUV_MEAN / max(C_base, k_boltz))**0.5 * 10.0, 2500.0)\n                \n                # Simple P(T) model: power law between T_min and T_peak\n                # P(T) \u221d T^(-alpha) with alpha ~ 1 + beta\n                # (grains cool fast at high T, so spend less time there)\n                alpha = 1.0 + pop.beta_FIR\n                \n                mask = (T_grid >= max(T_eq_grain * 0.5, 5.0)) & (T_grid <= T_peak)\n                if np.any(mask):\n                    P_T[i, mask] = T_grid[mask]**(-alpha)\n                    # Add peak at equilibrium\n                    idx_eq = np.argmin(np.abs(T_grid - T_eq_grain))\n                    P_T[i, idx_eq] += 10.0 * T_eq_grain**(-alpha)\n                else:\n                    idx_eq = np.argmin(np.abs(T_grid - T_eq_grain))\n                    P_T[i, idx_eq] = 1.0\n            \n            # Normalize\n            norm = np.sum(P_T[i])\n            if norm > 0:\n                P_T[i] /= norm\n        \n        return T_grid, P_T\n    \n    # --------------------------------------------------------\n    # Dust SED\n    # --------------------------------------------------------\n    \n    def compute_SED(self, G0, A_V=0, wavelengths=None):\n        \"\"\"\n        Compute the dust emission SED for one cell.\n        \n        Returns emissivity j_\u03bd per H nucleus at each wavelength.\n        \n        Parameters\n        ----------\n        G0 : float\n            FUV field [Habing]\n        A_V : float\n            Visual extinction [mag]\n        wavelengths : ndarray, optional\n            Wavelengths [cm]. Default: 1 \u03bcm \u2013 1 mm.\n        \n        Returns\n        -------\n        wavelengths : ndarray [cm]\n        j_nu : ndarray [erg s\u207b\u00b9 Hz\u207b\u00b9 sr\u207b\u00b9 per H]\n        \"\"\"\n        if wavelengths is None:\n            wavelengths = np.logspace(np.log10(1e-4), np.log10(0.1), 100)\n        \n        nu = c_light / wavelengths\n        j_nu = np.zeros_like(nu)\n        \n        # Large grains: thermal equilibrium emission\n        T_eq = self.equilibrium_temperature(G0, A_V)\n        \n        for pop in [self.large_C, self.silicate]:\n            T_arr = T_eq[pop.name]\n            for i in range(pop.n_bins):\n                Q_abs = pop.Q_abs_lambda(wavelengths)\n                sigma = np.pi * pop.a[i]**2\n                dn_da_i = pop.dn_da[i]\n                da_i = pop.da[i]\n                \n                # Planck function B_\u03bd(T)\n                T_i = T_arr[i]\n                x = h_planck * nu / (k_boltz * T_i)\n                x = np.clip(x, 0, 500)\n                B_nu = 2 * h_planck * nu**3 / c_light**2 / (np.exp(x) - 1 + 1e-30)\n                \n                j_nu += sigma * Q_abs * B_nu * dn_da_i * da_i\n        \n        # Nano-grains: stochastic emission\n        T_grid, P_T = self.stochastic_temperature_distribution(G0, A_V)\n        pop = self.nano_C\n        \n        for i in range(pop.n_bins):\n            Q_abs = pop.Q_abs_lambda(wavelengths)\n            sigma = np.pi * pop.a[i]**2\n            dn_da_i = pop.dn_da[i]\n            da_i = pop.da[i]\n            \n            # Integrate over P(T)\n            for j in range(len(T_grid)):\n                if P_T[i, j] <= 0:\n                    continue\n                T_j = T_grid[j]\n                x = h_planck * nu / (k_boltz * T_j)\n                x = np.clip(x, 0, 500)\n                B_nu = 2 * h_planck * nu**3 / c_light**2 / (np.exp(x) - 1 + 1e-30)\n                \n                dT = T_grid[min(j+1, len(T_grid)-1)] - T_grid[max(j-1, 0)]\n                j_nu += sigma * Q_abs * B_nu * P_T[i, j] * dn_da_i * da_i\n        \n        return wavelengths, j_nu\n    \n    # --------------------------------------------------------\n    # Dust evolution\n    # --------------------------------------------------------\n    \n    def evolve(self, G0, n_H, dt):\n        \"\"\"\n        Evolve the dust population based on local conditions.\n        \n        Implements two key THEMIS evolution processes:\n        1. Photo-destruction of nano-grains by FUV (Elyajouri+ 2024)\n        2. Band gap evolution (UV aromatization)\n        \n        Parameters\n        ----------\n        G0 : float\n            FUV field [Habing]\n        n_H : float\n            Density [cm\u207b\u00b3] (for accretion in dense regions)\n        dt : float\n            Time step [s]\n        \n        Returns\n        -------\n        f_nano_new : float\n            Updated nano-grain fraction\n        E_g_new : float\n            Updated band gap [eV]\n        \"\"\"\n        # 1. Photo-destruction of nano-carbons\n        # Timescale from Elyajouri+ (2024): \u03c4_dest ~ 10\u2074 yr at G0 = 10\u2074\n        # \u03c4 \u221d 1/G0 for photodestruction\n        tau_dest = 1e4 * 3.15e7 / max(G0 / 1e4, 0.01)  # seconds\n        \n        # Destruction rate\n        f_dest = np.exp(-dt / tau_dest)\n        \n        # 2. Replenishment from large grain fragmentation\n        # Shattering in turbulence or UV-driven fragmentation\n        # Timescale much longer: ~10\u2076 yr\n        tau_replenish = 1e6 * 3.15e7\n        f_replenish = min(dt / tau_replenish, 0.1)  # Slow\n        \n        # 3. In dense shielded gas (G0 < 1, n > 10\u2074):\n        # Nano-grains can re-form via accretion of C from gas\n        if G0 < 1 and n_H > 1e4:\n            tau_accrete = 1e5 * 3.15e7 * (1e4 / n_H)\n            f_accrete = min(dt / tau_accrete, 0.1)\n        else:\n            f_accrete = 0.0\n        \n        # Update f_nano\n        f_nano_new = self.f_nano * f_dest + (1.0 - self.f_nano) * f_replenish + f_accrete\n        f_nano_new = np.clip(f_nano_new, 0.01, 2.0)  # Floor and ceiling\n        \n        # 4. Band gap evolution: UV lowers E_g (aromatization)\n        # In strong FUV: E_g \u2192 0 on timescale ~10\u00b3 yr\n        # In shielded regions: E_g stays high or increases (hydrogenation)\n        tau_aromat = 1e3 * 3.15e7 / max(G0, 0.01)\n        E_g_eq = 0.01 if G0 > 10 else min(0.1 + 0.5 * np.log10(max(n_H, 1)), 2.5)\n        \n        E_g_new = self.E_g + (E_g_eq - self.E_g) * min(dt / tau_aromat, 1.0)\n        E_g_new = np.clip(E_g_new, 0.01, 2.5)\n        \n        return f_nano_new, E_g_new\n    \n    # --------------------------------------------------------\n    # FUV extinction\n    # --------------------------------------------------------\n    \n    def sigma_FUV_per_H(self):\n        \"\"\"Total FUV dust cross section per H nucleus [cm\u00b2].\"\"\"\n        return sum(pop.cross_section_FUV() for pop in self.populations)\n    \n    def A_V_per_NH(self):\n        \"\"\"Visual extinction per H column [mag cm\u00b2].\"\"\"\n        sigma_V = sum(\n            np.sum(np.pi * pop.a**2 * pop.Q_abs_V * pop.dn_da * pop.da)\n            for pop in self.populations\n        )\n        return sigma_V / 1.086  # Convert optical depth to mag\n    \n    # --------------------------------------------------------\n    # Electron balance (PAH-assisted recombination)\n    # --------------------------------------------------------\n    \n    def grain_electron_recombination_rate(self, T, G0, x_e, n_H):\n        \"\"\"\n        Electron recombination rate on grain surfaces.\n        \n        Nano-carbons (PAH analogs) are the dominant electron sink\n        in the surface layers of PDRs.\n        \n        Returns rate coefficient k_gr such that\n        dn_e/dt_grain = -k_gr \u00d7 n_e \u00d7 n_H [cm\u207b\u00b3 s\u207b\u00b9]\n        \"\"\"\n        pop = self.nano_C\n        n_e = max(x_e, 1e-10) * n_H\n        v_e = np.sqrt(8 * k_boltz * T / (np.pi * m_e))\n        \n        k_total = 0.0\n        for i in range(pop.n_bins):\n            a = pop.a[i]\n            sigma = np.pi * a**2\n            \n            # Grain charge (simplified \u2014 use mean Z from PE calculation)\n            psi = G0 * np.sqrt(T) / n_e if n_e > 0 else 1e10\n            Z = min(psi * a / 1e-7, 10)  # Very rough\n            \n            # Electron sticking enhanced by Coulomb for Z > 0\n            if Z > 0:\n                S_e = 1.0 + Z * e_charge**2 / (a * k_boltz * T)\n            else:\n                S_e = max(1.0 + Z * e_charge**2 / (a * k_boltz * T), 0.0)\n            \n            k_total += v_e * sigma * S_e * pop.dn_da[i] * pop.da[i]\n        \n        return k_total\n    \n    # --------------------------------------------------------\n    # Summary / diagnostics\n    # --------------------------------------------------------\n    \n    def summary(self):\n        \"\"\"Print a summary of the dust model state.\"\"\"\n        print(f\"THEMIS Dust Model:\")\n        print(f\"  Nano-grain fraction: {self.f_nano:.3f} (1.0 = diffuse ISM)\")\n        print(f\"  Band gap E_g: {self.E_g:.2f} eV\")\n        print(f\"  \u03c3_FUV per H: {self.sigma_FUV_per_H():.2e} cm\u00b2\")\n        print(f\"  A_V/N_H: {self.A_V_per_NH():.2e} mag cm\u00b2\")\n        for pop in self.populations:\n            print(f\"  {pop.name}: n/H = {pop.total_number():.2e}, \"\n                  f\"area/H = {pop.total_surface_area():.2e} cm\u00b2/H, \"\n                  f\"mass/H = {pop.mass_per_H():.2e} g/H\")\n        print(f\"  R_H2 (T=50K, Td=20K): {self.h2_formation_rate(50, 20):.2e} cm\u00b3/s\")\n",
      "path": "/home/claude/prism3d/grains/themis.py"
    },
    "message": "THEMIS dust model for PRISM-3D \u2014 the core multi-component grain module with stochastic heating, PE heating from charge distribution, dust evolution, and SED computation",

    "display_content": {
      "type": "code_block",
      "language": "python",
      "code": "\"\"\"\nTHEMIS Dust Model for PRISM-3D.\n\nImplements the Heterogeneous dust Evolution Model for Interstellar Solids\n(Jones et al. 2013, 2017; Ysard et al. 2024 \u2014 THEMIS 2.0) adapted for\nself-consistent coupling to PDR gas chemistry and thermal balance.\n\nThree grain populations:\n  1. a-C(:H) nano-particles (a = 0.4\u201320 nm): aromatic/aliphatic carbon\n     \u2192 stochastically heated, produce MIR AIB emission\n     \u2192 dominate photoelectric heating\n     \u2192 evolve via UV photo-processing (aromatization, destruction)\n  2. Large a-C(:H) grains (a = 20 nm \u2013 0.2 \u03bcm): carbonaceous grains\n     \u2192 in thermal equilibrium\n     \u2192 contribute to FUV/visible extinction\n  3. Large a-Sil grains (a = 20 nm \u2013 0.3 \u03bcm): amorphous silicates\n     \u2192 in thermal equilibrium\n     \u2192 dominate FIR/submm emission\n\nKey physics:\n  - Band gap E_g controls aromatic vs aliphatic character\n  - UV processing lowers E_g (aliphatic \u2192 aromatic)\n  - Photo-destruction of nano-particles in strong FUV\n  - Size-dependent optical properties Q_abs(a, \u03bb)\n  - Full PE heating from charge distribution (replaces BT94)\n  - Stochastic heating via temperature probability distribution P(T)\n  - Feedback to gas: PE heating, H\u2082 formation, electron balance, UV opacity\n\nReferences:\n  Jones et al. 2013, A&A 558, A62 \u2014 THEMIS framework\n  Jones et al. 2017, A&A 602, A46 \u2014 Global THEMIS model\n  Compi\u00e8gne et al. 2011, A&A 525, A103 \u2014 DustEM code\n  Elyajouri et al. 2024, A&A 685, A76 \u2014 JWST dust evolution Orion Bar\n  Ysard et al. 2024 \u2014 THEMIS 2.0\n\"\"\"\n\nimport numpy as np\nfrom ..utils.constants import (\n    k_boltz, h_planck, c_light, sigma_SB, eV_to_erg,\n    e_charge, m_H, m_e, pc_cm\n)\n\n\n# ============================================================\n# Physical constants for dust\n# ============================================================\n\n# Carbon atom mass\nm_C = 12.0 * m_H  # g\n\n# Debye specific heat: C_grain \u2248 N_atoms \u00d7 k_B at high T\n# At low T, C \u221d T\u00b3 (Debye model)\nDEBYE_T_CARBON = 863.0  # K, Debye temperature for graphite\nDEBYE_T_SILICATE = 500.0  # K, Debye temperature for silicates\n\n# FUV energy range\nE_FUV_MIN = 6.0 * eV_to_erg   # 6 eV\nE_FUV_MAX = 13.6 * eV_to_erg  # 13.6 eV\nE_FUV_MEAN = 10.0 * eV_to_erg # Mean FUV photon energy\n\n# Standard ISRF FUV flux (Habing field)\nF_HABING = 1.6e-3  # erg cm\u207b\u00b2 s\u207b\u00b9 (6-13.6 eV)\n\n# Dust-to-gas mass ratio (standard ISM)\nDUST_TO_GAS_STANDARD = 0.01\n\n# THEMIS band gap for diffuse ISM carbon nano-grains\nEG_DIFFUSE = 0.1   # eV \u2014 partially aromatic (UV-processed in diffuse ISM)\nEG_DENSE = 2.5     # eV \u2014 highly aliphatic (shielded in dense regions)\nEG_AROMATIC = 0.0   # eV \u2014 fully aromatic (graphitic)\n\n\n# ============================================================\n# THEMIS Grain Population\n# ============================================================\n\nclass THEMISPopulation:\n    \"\"\"\n    A single THEMIS grain population (nano-carbons, large carbons, or silicates).\n    \n    Each population has a size distribution, material properties, and\n    optical properties that depend on size and band gap.\n    \n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    name : str\n        Population name ('aC_nano', 'aC_large', 'aSil')\n    a_min : float\n        Minimum grain radius [cm]\n    a_max : float\n        Maximum grain radius [cm]\n    n_bins : int\n        Number of size bins\n    rho : float\n        Material density [g/cm\u00b3]\n    E_g : float\n        Band gap [eV] \u2014 controls aromatic/aliphatic character\n        Only meaningful for carbonaceous grains.\n    slope : float\n        Power-law slope of dn/da (default -3.5 for MRN)\n    mass_fraction : float\n        Fraction of total dust mass in this population\n    \"\"\"\n    \n    def __init__(self, name, a_min, a_max, n_bins=15,\n                 rho=2.24, E_g=0.1, slope=-3.5, mass_fraction=0.1):\n        self.name = name\n        self.a_min = a_min\n        self.a_max = a_max\n        self.n_bins = n_bins\n        self.rho = rho\n        self.E_g = E_g  # Band gap [eV]\n        self.slope = slope\n        self.mass_fraction = mass_fraction\n        \n        # Size grid\n        self.a_edges = np.logspace(np.log10(a_min), np.log10(a_max), n_bins + 1)\n        self.a = np.sqrt(self.a_edges[:-1] * self.a_edges[1:])  # geometric mean\n        self.da = self.a_edges[1:] - self.a_edges[:-1]\n        \n        # Size distribution dn/da per H nucleus [cm\u207b\u00b9 per H]\n        raw = self.a**slope\n        # Normalize to target mass fraction\n        mass_integral = np.sum((4./3.) * np.pi * self.a**3 * rho * raw * self.da)\n        target_mass = mass_fraction * DUST_TO_GAS_STANDARD * m_H\n        self.dn_da = raw * (target_mass / mass_integral) if mass_integral > 0 else raw * 0\n        \n        # Number of carbon atoms per grain (for nano-carbons)\n        self.N_C = (4./3.) * np.pi * self.a**3 * rho / m_C\n        \n        # Precompute optical properties\n        self._compute_optics()\n    \n    def _compute_optics(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Compute absorption and scattering efficiencies.\n        \n        Uses analytic approximations calibrated to THEMIS/DustEM tables.\n        Q_abs depends on size, wavelength, and band gap.\n        \"\"\"\n        # FUV absorption efficiency (integrated 6-13.6 eV)\n        # Small grains: Q_abs \u2248 1 (geometric limit for a > \u03bb/2\u03c0)\n        # Very small grains: Q_abs \u221d a (Rayleigh regime)\n        lambda_FUV = 0.1e-4  # ~1000 \u00c5 = 0.1 \u03bcm representative FUV wavelength\n        x = 2 * np.pi * self.a / lambda_FUV  # size parameter\n        \n        # Carbonaceous grains (higher FUV absorption than silicates)\n        if 'aC' in self.name:\n            self.Q_abs_FUV = np.where(x > 1, 1.0, x)\n            # Band gap effect: lower E_g \u2192 more UV absorption\n            E_g_factor = 1.0 + 0.5 * (0.1 - min(self.E_g, 0.5))\n            self.Q_abs_FUV *= np.clip(E_g_factor, 0.5, 1.5)\n        else:\n            # Silicates: lower FUV efficiency\n            self.Q_abs_FUV = np.where(x > 1, 0.7, 0.7 * x)\n        \n        # Optical/NIR absorption (for extinction curve)\n        lambda_V = 0.55e-4  # V band\n        x_V = 2 * np.pi * self.a / lambda_V\n        self.Q_abs_V = np.where(x_V > 1, 1.0, x_V**2)\n        \n        # FIR/submm emissivity: Q_abs \u221d a * (\u03bd/\u03bd\u2080)^\u03b2\n        # \u03b2 \u2248 2 for silicates, 1.5-2 for amorphous carbon\n        self.beta_FIR = 2.0 if 'Sil' in self.name else 1.8\n        \n        # Reference: Q_abs at 250 \u03bcm for a = 0.1 \u03bcm grain\n        self.Q_abs_250_ref = 1e-3 if 'Sil' in self.name else 5e-4\n    \n    def Q_abs_lambda(self, wavelength_cm):\n        \"\"\"\n        Absorption efficiency at a given wavelength for all size bins.\n        \n        Parameters\n        ----------\n        wavelength_cm : float\n            Wavelength [cm]\n        \n        Returns\n        -------\n        Q_abs : ndarray, shape (n_bins,)\n        \"\"\"\n        x = 2 * np.pi * self.a / wavelength_cm\n        \n        if wavelength_cm < 0.3e-4:  # UV\n            return self.Q_abs_FUV\n        elif wavelength_cm < 5e-4:  # Optical/NIR\n            return np.where(x > 1, 1.0, x**2)\n        else:  # FIR/submm\n            nu = c_light / wavelength_cm\n            nu_ref = c_light / 250e-4  # 250 \u03bcm reference\n            Q_ref = self.Q_abs_250_ref * (self.a / 1e-5)  # scales with a\n            return Q_ref * (nu / nu_ref)**self.beta_FIR\n    \n    def cross_section_FUV(self):\n        \"\"\"Total FUV extinction cross section per H [cm\u00b2/H].\"\"\"\n        return np.sum(np.pi * self.a**2 * self.Q_abs_FUV * self.dn_da * self.da)\n    \n    def total_surface_area(self):\n        \"\"\"Total grain surface area per H [cm\u00b2/H].\"\"\"\n        return np.sum(4.0 * np.pi * self.a**2 * self.dn_da * self.da)\n    \n    def total_number(self):\n        \"\"\"Total grain number per H.\"\"\"\n        return np.sum(self.dn_da * self.da)\n    \n    def mass_per_H(self):\n        \"\"\"Total grain mass per H nucleus [g/H].\"\"\"\n        return np.sum((4./3.) * np.pi * self.a**3 * self.rho * self.dn_da * self.da)\n\n\n# ============================================================\n# Full THEMIS Dust Model\n# ============================================================\n\nclass THEMISDust:\n    \"\"\"\n    Complete THEMIS dust model with three populations.\n    \n    Provides:\n    - Photoelectric heating from grain charge distribution\n    - H\u2082 formation on grain surfaces\n    - Dust temperature (equilibrium for large, stochastic for nano)\n    - Dust SED per cell\n    - Dust evolution (nano-grain depletion by UV)\n    \n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    f_nano : float\n        Scaling factor for nano-grain abundance relative to diffuse ISM.\n        1.0 = standard, 0.07 = Orion Bar edge (Elyajouri+ 2024)\n    E_g : float\n        Band gap of nano-carbons [eV]. 0.1 = diffuse ISM, higher = denser/shielded\n    \"\"\"\n    \n    def __init__(self, f_nano=1.0, E_g=0.1):\n        self.f_nano = f_nano\n        self.E_g = E_g\n        \n        # Build three populations following THEMIS diffuse ISM model\n        # Mass fractions: ~25% in nano-C, ~8% in large a-C, ~67% in silicates\n        self.nano_C = THEMISPopulation(\n            'aC_nano',\n            a_min=4e-8,    # 0.4 nm\n            a_max=2e-7,    # 20 nm (nano-grain regime)\n            n_bins=12,\n            rho=1.6,       # g/cm\u00b3 for a-C:H (lower than graphite)\n            E_g=E_g,\n            slope=-3.5,\n            mass_fraction=0.25 * f_nano  # Depleted by f_nano\n        )\n        \n        self.large_C = THEMISPopulation(\n            'aC_large',\n            a_min=2e-7,    # 20 nm\n            a_max=2e-5,    # 0.2 \u03bcm\n            n_bins=10,\n            rho=1.6,\n            E_g=0.1,       # Large grains are always UV-processed on surfaces\n            slope=-3.5,\n            mass_fraction=0.08\n        )\n        \n        self.silicate = THEMISPopulation(\n            'aSil',\n            a_min=2e-7,    # 20 nm\n            a_max=3e-5,    # 0.3 \u03bcm\n            n_bins=10,\n            rho=3.5,\n            slope=-3.5,\n            mass_fraction=0.67\n        )\n        \n        self.populations = [self.nano_C, self.large_C, self.silicate]\n    \n    # --------------------------------------------------------\n    # Photoelectric heating\n    # --------------------------------------------------------\n    \n    def photoelectric_heating(self, n_H, T, G0, x_e):\n        \"\"\"\n        Compute PE heating from the full grain charge distribution.\n        \n        This replaces the Bakes & Tielens (1994) analytic fit with a\n        proper WD01-style calculation summing over all grain sizes.\n        The key improvement: accounts for nano-grain depletion and\n        band-gap-dependent yields.\n        \n        Parameters\n        ----------\n        n_H : float\n            H number density [cm\u207b\u00b3]\n        T : float\n            Gas temperature [K]\n        G0 : float\n            FUV field [Habing units]\n        x_e : float\n            Electron fraction\n        \n        Returns\n        -------\n        Gamma_PE : float\n            PE heating rate [erg cm\u207b\u00b3 s\u207b\u00b9]\n        \"\"\"\n        if G0 <= 0 or T <= 0:\n            return 0.0\n        \n        n_e = max(x_e, 1e-10) * n_H\n        Gamma_total = 0.0\n        \n        for pop in self.populations:\n            for i in range(pop.n_bins):\n                a = pop.a[i]\n                n_gr = pop.dn_da[i] * pop.da[i] * n_H  # grain number density\n                \n                if n_gr <= 0:\n                    continue\n                \n                # Grain charging parameter (WD01 Eq. 2)\n                psi = G0 * np.sqrt(T) / n_e\n                \n                # Mean grain charge Z(a, psi)\n                # Small grains can have Z ~ 0 or negative\n                # Large grains tend to be positively charged\n                N_C_grain = (4./3.) * np.pi * a**3 * pop.rho / m_C\n                \n                # Ionization potential (work function + charging)\n                # a-C(:H): IP depends on band gap\n                if 'aC' in pop.name:\n                    W = (4.0 + pop.E_g) * eV_to_erg  # Work function\n                    Y_0 = 0.14  # Base yield for a-C\n                else:\n                    W = 8.0 * eV_to_erg  # Silicate work function\n                    Y_0 = 0.05  # Lower yield for silicates\n                \n                # Mean charge from WD01 simplified charging balance\n                # Ionization rate \u221d G0 * sigma * Y\n                # Recombination rate \u221d n_e * v_e * sigma * S_e(Z)\n                v_e = np.sqrt(8 * k_boltz * T / (np.pi * m_e))\n                \n                # Solve for Z iteratively (quick: 3 iterations)\n                Z = 0.0\n                for _ in range(3):\n                    # PE yield decreases with positive charge\n                    Y = Y_0 * np.exp(-max(Z, 0) * e_charge**2 / (a * E_FUV_MEAN))\n                    Y = max(Y, 1e-4)\n                    \n                    # Electron sticking (Coulomb enhanced for Z > 0)\n                    S_e = 1.0 if Z <= 0 else 1.0 + Z * e_charge**2 / (a * k_boltz * T)\n                    \n                    # Charge balance: G0 * sigma_abs * phi_UV * Y = n_e * v_e * pi*a\u00b2 * S_e\n                    phi_UV = F_HABING * G0 / E_FUV_MEAN\n                    sigma_abs = np.pi * a**2 * pop.Q_abs_FUV[i]\n                    \n                    R_ion = sigma_abs * phi_UV * Y\n                    R_rec = n_e * v_e * np.pi * a**2 * S_e\n                    \n                    if R_rec > 0:\n                        Z = R_ion / R_rec - 0.5  # Approximate\n                        Z = max(Z, -2.0)  # Can't be very negative\n                \n                # PE electron energy\n                E_pe = max(E_FUV_MEAN - W - Z * e_charge**2 / max(a, 1e-8), 0)\n                \n                # Heating rate from this grain\n                Gamma_i = n_gr * sigma_abs * phi_UV * Y * E_pe\n                Gamma_total += Gamma_i\n        \n        return Gamma_total\n    \n    # --------------------------------------------------------\n    # H\u2082 formation\n    # --------------------------------------------------------\n    \n    def h2_formation_rate(self, T, T_dust):\n        \"\"\"\n        H\u2082 formation rate coefficient on all grain surfaces [cm\u00b3/s].\n        \n        Accounts for:\n        - Size-dependent surface area\n        - Temperature-dependent sticking\n        - Chemisorbed sites on carbonaceous grains (high-T formation)\n        - Physisorbed sites on silicates (low-T formation)\n        \n        Returns R such that formation rate = R \u00d7 n(H) \u00d7 n_H [cm\u207b\u00b3 s\u207b\u00b9]\n        \"\"\"\n        v_H = np.sqrt(8.0 * k_boltz * T / (np.pi * m_H))\n        \n        # Sticking coefficient (Hollenbach & McKee 1979, updated)\n        S_H = 1.0 / (1.0 + 0.04 * np.sqrt(T + T_dust)\n                     + 0.002 * T + 8e-6 * T**2)\n        \n        R_total = 0.0\n        \n        for pop in self.populations:\n            for i in range(pop.n_bins):\n                sigma = np.pi * pop.a[i]**2\n                dn_da_i = pop.dn_da[i]\n                da_i = pop.da[i]\n                \n                # Surface recombination efficiency\n                if 'Sil' in pop.name:\n                    # Silicates: physisorption-dominated\n                    # Efficient at T_dust < 20 K, drops sharply above\n                    if T_dust < 14:\n                        eps = 1.0\n                    elif T_dust < 25:\n                        eps = np.exp(-(T_dust - 14) / 5.0)\n                    else:\n                        eps = 0.01\n                else:\n                    # Carbonaceous: chemisorption allows high-T formation\n                    # Cazaux & Tielens (2004): efficient up to ~400 K\n                    if T_dust < 50:\n                        eps = 0.8\n                    elif T_dust < 400:\n                        eps = 0.8 * np.exp(-(T_dust - 50) / 200.0)\n                    else:\n                        eps = 0.01\n                    # Eley-Rideal mechanism at very high T\n                    # (KOSMA-\u03c4 2022 update)\n                    eps = max(eps, 0.005)\n                \n                R_total += 0.5 * v_H * S_H * eps * sigma * dn_da_i * da_i\n        \n        return R_total\n    \n    # --------------------------------------------------------\n    # Dust temperature\n    # --------------------------------------------------------\n    \n    def equilibrium_temperature(self, G0, A_V=0):\n        \"\"\"\n        Equilibrium dust temperature for large grains.\n        \n        Balances FUV/optical absorption against FIR emission.\n        Returns dict of {population_name: T_dust_array}.\n        \"\"\"\n        T_eq = {}\n        \n        for pop in [self.large_C, self.silicate]:\n            # Absorbed power per grain: sigma_abs * F_UV * exp(-1.8 AV)\n            # + optical/NIR ISRF contribution\n            F_abs = F_HABING * G0 * np.exp(-1.8 * A_V)\n            # Add optical ISRF (roughly equal to FUV in energy)\n            F_abs += F_HABING * 0.5 * np.exp(-A_V)\n            \n            P_abs = np.pi * pop.a**2 * pop.Q_abs_FUV * F_abs\n            \n            # Emitted power: 4\u03c0 * sigma_SB * T^4 * <Q_abs>_Planck * 4\u03c0 a\u00b2\n            # <Q_abs>_Planck ~ Q_0 * (a/a_0) * (T/T_0)^beta\n            # Solving: T = [P_abs / (16\u03c0\u00b2 \u03c3_SB a\u00b2 Q_eff)]^(1/(4+beta))\n            \n            beta = pop.beta_FIR\n            Q_eff_ref = pop.Q_abs_250_ref  # at 250 \u03bcm, a = 0.1 \u03bcm, T ~ 20K\n            # Scale: Q_eff(a,T) \u2248 Q_ref * (a/1e-5) * (T/20)^beta\n            # This gives: P_abs = 16\u03c0\u00b2 \u03c3_SB a\u00b2 Q_ref (a/1e-5) T^(4+beta) / 20^beta\n            \n            denom = 16 * np.pi**2 * sigma_SB * pop.a**2 * Q_eff_ref * (pop.a / 1e-5) / 20.0**beta\n            denom = np.maximum(denom, 1e-50)\n            \n            T = np.power(P_abs / denom, 1.0 / (4.0 + beta))\n            T = np.clip(T, 5.0, 2000.0)  # Physical bounds\n            \n            T_eq[pop.name] = T\n        \n        return T_eq\n    \n    def stochastic_temperature_distribution(self, G0, A_V=0, n_T_bins=50):\n        \"\"\"\n        Temperature probability distribution P(T) for nano-grains.\n        \n        Small grains undergo temperature spikes from individual\n        UV photon absorptions. The time-averaged P(T) determines\n        the MIR emission spectrum (PAH bands, VSG continuum).\n        \n        Uses the method of Draine & Li (2001): solve the thermal\n        balance with discretized temperature bins.\n        \n        Returns\n        -------\n        T_grid : ndarray (n_T_bins,)\n        P_T : ndarray (n_bins, n_T_bins)\n            P(T) for each size bin of nano-carbons\n        \"\"\"\n        T_grid = np.logspace(np.log10(5.0), np.log10(2500.0), n_T_bins)\n        pop = self.nano_C\n        P_T = np.zeros((pop.n_bins, n_T_bins))\n        \n        F_abs = F_HABING * G0 * np.exp(-1.8 * A_V)\n        phi_UV = F_abs / E_FUV_MEAN  # FUV photon flux [cm\u207b\u00b2 s\u207b\u00b9]\n        \n        for i in range(pop.n_bins):\n            a = pop.a[i]\n            N_C = pop.N_C[i]\n            \n            if N_C < 10:\n                N_C = 10  # Minimum for meaningful heat capacity\n            \n            # Photon absorption rate\n            sigma_abs = np.pi * a**2 * pop.Q_abs_FUV[i]\n            R_abs = sigma_abs * phi_UV  # photons/s\n            \n            if R_abs <= 0:\n                P_T[i, 0] = 1.0\n                continue\n            \n            # Heat capacity: C(T) = N_atoms * k_B * f_Debye(T/T_D)\n            # Simplified: C \u2248 N_C * k_B * min(1, (T/T_D)\u00b3) for carbon\n            T_D = DEBYE_T_CARBON\n            C_grain = N_C * k_boltz * np.minimum(1.0, (T_grid / T_D)**3)\n            C_grain = np.maximum(C_grain, k_boltz)  # Floor\n            \n            # Temperature after absorbing one FUV photon\n            # \u0394T \u2248 E_photon / C(T_base)\n            # For small grains: T_peak can be >> T_eq\n            \n            # Cooling rate: dE/dt = -4\u03c0 a\u00b2 \u03c3_SB <Q_abs> T\u2074\n            # \u2248 4\u03c0 a\u00b2 * Q_eff * \u03c3_SB * T^(4+beta)\n            Q_eff = pop.Q_abs_250_ref * (a / 1e-5)\n            cool_rate = 4 * np.pi * a**2 * Q_eff * sigma_SB * T_grid**(4 + pop.beta_FIR) / 20.0**pop.beta_FIR\n            \n            # Cooling time: t_cool = C * T / cool_rate\n            t_cool = C_grain * T_grid / np.maximum(cool_rate, 1e-50)\n            \n            # Time between photon absorptions\n            t_abs = 1.0 / max(R_abs, 1e-30)\n            \n            # Steady-state P(T): balance heating spikes with cooling\n            # Grains spend most time near T_min (just before next photon)\n            # P(T) \u221d t_cool(T) for T near equilibrium,\n            # with spikes extending to T_peak after absorption\n            \n            # Equilibrium temperature (for reference)\n            P_abs_grain = sigma_abs * F_abs\n            denom_eq = 4 * np.pi * a**2 * Q_eff * sigma_SB / 20.0**pop.beta_FIR\n            T_eq_grain = max((P_abs_grain / max(denom_eq, 1e-50))**(1.0/(4+pop.beta_FIR)), 5.0)\n            \n            # For very small grains (N_C < 200): truly stochastic\n            # For larger grains: P(T) \u2192 delta(T - T_eq)\n            if N_C > 500 or t_abs > 100 * t_cool[n_T_bins // 2]:\n                # Thermal equilibrium regime\n                idx_eq = np.argmin(np.abs(T_grid - T_eq_grain))\n                P_T[i, idx_eq] = 1.0\n            else:\n                # Stochastic regime\n                # T_peak after one photon absorption from T_base ~ 5-10 K\n                C_base = N_C * k_boltz * min(1.0, (10.0 / T_D)**3)\n                T_peak = min((E_FUV_MEAN / max(C_base, k_boltz))**0.5 * 10.0, 2500.0)\n                \n                # Simple P(T) model: power law between T_min and T_peak\n                # P(T) \u221d T^(-alpha) with alpha ~ 1 + beta\n                # (grains cool fast at high T, so spend less time there)\n                alpha = 1.0 + pop.beta_FIR\n                \n                mask = (T_grid >= max(T_eq_grain * 0.5, 5.0)) & (T_grid <= T_peak)\n                if np.any(mask):\n                    P_T[i, mask] = T_grid[mask]**(-alpha)\n                    # Add peak at equilibrium\n                    idx_eq = np.argmin(np.abs(T_grid - T_eq_grain))\n                    P_T[i, idx_eq] += 10.0 * T_eq_grain**(-alpha)\n                else:\n                    idx_eq = np.argmin(np.abs(T_grid - T_eq_grain))\n                    P_T[i, idx_eq] = 1.0\n            \n            # Normalize\n            norm = np.sum(P_T[i])\n            if norm > 0:\n                P_T[i] /= norm\n        \n        return T_grid, P_T\n    \n    # --------------------------------------------------------\n    # Dust SED\n    # --------------------------------------------------------\n    \n    def compute_SED(self, G0, A_V=0, wavelengths=None):\n        \"\"\"\n        Compute the dust emission SED for one cell.\n        \n        Returns emissivity j_\u03bd per H nucleus at each wavelength.\n        \n        Parameters\n        ----------\n        G0 : float\n            FUV field [Habing]\n        A_V : float\n            Visual extinction [mag]\n        wavelengths : ndarray, optional\n            Wavelengths [cm]. Default: 1 \u03bcm \u2013 1 mm.\n        \n        Returns\n        -------\n        wavelengths : ndarray [cm]\n        j_nu : ndarray [erg s\u207b\u00b9 Hz\u207b\u00b9 sr\u207b\u00b9 per H]\n        \"\"\"\n        if wavelengths is None:\n            wavelengths = np.logspace(np.log10(1e-4), np.log10(0.1), 100)\n        \n        nu = c_light / wavelengths\n        j_nu = np.zeros_like(nu)\n        \n        # Large grains: thermal equilibrium emission\n        T_eq = self.equilibrium_temperature(G0, A_V)\n        \n        for pop in [self.large_C, self.silicate]:\n            T_arr = T_eq[pop.name]\n            for i in range(pop.n_bins):\n                Q_abs = pop.Q_abs_lambda(wavelengths)\n                sigma = np.pi * pop.a[i]**2\n                dn_da_i = pop.dn_da[i]\n                da_i = pop.da[i]\n                \n                # Planck function B_\u03bd(T)\n                T_i = T_arr[i]\n                x = h_planck * nu / (k_boltz * T_i)\n                x = np.clip(x, 0, 500)\n                B_nu = 2 * h_planck * nu**3 / c_light**2 / (np.exp(x) - 1 + 1e-30)\n                \n                j_nu += sigma * Q_abs * B_nu * dn_da_i * da_i\n        \n        # Nano-grains: stochastic emission\n        T_grid, P_T = self.stochastic_temperature_distribution(G0, A_V)\n        pop = self.nano_C\n        \n        for i in range(pop.n_bins):\n            Q_abs = pop.Q_abs_lambda(wavelengths)\n            sigma = np.pi * pop.a[i]**2\n            dn_da_i = pop.dn_da[i]\n            da_i = pop.da[i]\n            \n            # Integrate over P(T)\n            for j in range(len(T_grid)):\n                if P_T[i, j] <= 0:\n                    continue\n                T_j = T_grid[j]\n                x = h_planck * nu / (k_boltz * T_j)\n                x = np.clip(x, 0, 500)\n                B_nu = 2 * h_planck * nu**3 / c_light**2 / (np.exp(x) - 1 + 1e-30)\n                \n                dT = T_grid[min(j+1, len(T_grid)-1)] - T_grid[max(j-1, 0)]\n                j_nu += sigma * Q_abs * B_nu * P_T[i, j] * dn_da_i * da_i\n        \n        return wavelengths, j_nu\n    \n    # --------------------------------------------------------\n    # Dust evolution\n    # --------------------------------------------------------\n    \n    def evolve(self, G0, n_H, dt):\n        \"\"\"\n        Evolve the dust population based on local conditions.\n        \n        Implements two key THEMIS evolution processes:\n        1. Photo-destruction of nano-grains by FUV (Elyajouri+ 2024)\n        2. Band gap evolution (UV aromatization)\n        \n        Parameters\n        ----------\n        G0 : float\n            FUV field [Habing]\n        n_H : float\n            Density [cm\u207b\u00b3] (for accretion in dense regions)\n        dt : float\n            Time step [s]\n        \n        Returns\n        -------\n        f_nano_new : float\n            Updated nano-grain fraction\n        E_g_new : float\n            Updated band gap [eV]\n        \"\"\"\n        # 1. Photo-destruction of nano-carbons\n        # Timescale from Elyajouri+ (2024): \u03c4_dest ~ 10\u2074 yr at G0 = 10\u2074\n        # \u03c4 \u221d 1/G0 for photodestruction\n        tau_dest = 1e4 * 3.15e7 / max(G0 / 1e4, 0.01)  # seconds\n        \n        # Destruction rate\n        f_dest = np.exp(-dt / tau_dest)\n        \n        # 2. Replenishment from large grain fragmentation\n        # Shattering in turbulence or UV-driven fragmentation\n        # Timescale much longer: ~10\u2076 yr\n        tau_replenish = 1e6 * 3.15e7\n        f_replenish = min(dt / tau_replenish, 0.1)  # Slow\n        \n        # 3. In dense shielded gas (G0 < 1, n > 10\u2074):\n        # Nano-grains can re-form via accretion of C from gas\n        if G0 < 1 and n_H > 1e4:\n            tau_accrete = 1e5 * 3.15e7 * (1e4 / n_H)\n            f_accrete = min(dt / tau_accrete, 0.1)\n        else:\n            f_accrete = 0.0\n        \n        # Update f_nano\n        f_nano_new = self.f_nano * f_dest + (1.0 - self.f_nano) * f_replenish + f_accrete\n        f_nano_new = np.clip(f_nano_new, 0.01, 2.0)  # Floor and ceiling\n        \n        # 4. Band gap evolution: UV lowers E_g (aromatization)\n        # In strong FUV: E_g \u2192 0 on timescale ~10\u00b3 yr\n        # In shielded regions: E_g stays high or increases (hydrogenation)\n        tau_aromat = 1e3 * 3.15e7 / max(G0, 0.01)\n        E_g_eq = 0.01 if G0 > 10 else min(0.1 + 0.5 * np.log10(max(n_H, 1)), 2.5)\n        \n        E_g_new = self.E_g + (E_g_eq - self.E_g) * min(dt / tau_aromat, 1.0)\n        E_g_new = np.clip(E_g_new, 0.01, 2.5)\n        \n        return f_nano_new, E_g_new\n    \n    # --------------------------------------------------------\n    # FUV extinction\n    # --------------------------------------------------------\n    \n    def sigma_FUV_per_H(self):\n        \"\"\"Total FUV dust cross section per H nucleus [cm\u00b2].\"\"\"\n        return sum(pop.cross_section_FUV() for pop in self.populations)\n    \n    def A_V_per_NH(self):\n        \"\"\"Visual extinction per H column [mag cm\u00b2].\"\"\"\n        sigma_V = sum(\n            np.sum(np.pi * pop.a**2 * pop.Q_abs_V * pop.dn_da * pop.da)\n            for pop in self.populations\n        )\n        return sigma_V / 1.086  # Convert optical depth to mag\n    \n    # --------------------------------------------------------\n    # Electron balance (PAH-assisted recombination)\n    # --------------------------------------------------------\n    \n    def grain_electron_recombination_rate(self, T, G0, x_e, n_H):\n        \"\"\"\n        Electron recombination rate on grain surfaces.\n        \n        Nano-carbons (PAH analogs) are the dominant electron sink\n        in the surface layers of PDRs.\n        \n        Returns rate coefficient k_gr such that\n        dn_e/dt_grain = -k_gr \u00d7 n_e \u00d7 n_H [cm\u207b\u00b3 s\u207b\u00b9]\n        \"\"\"\n        pop = self.nano_C\n        n_e = max(x_e, 1e-10) * n_H\n        v_e = np.sqrt(8 * k_boltz * T / (np.pi * m_e))\n        \n        k_total = 0.0\n        for i in range(pop.n_bins):\n            a = pop.a[i]\n            sigma = np.pi * a**2\n            \n            # Grain charge (simplified \u2014 use mean Z from PE calculation)\n            psi = G0 * np.sqrt(T) / n_e if n_e > 0 else 1e10\n            Z = min(psi * a / 1e-7, 10)  # Very rough\n            \n            # Electron sticking enhanced by Coulomb for Z > 0\n            if Z > 0:\n                S_e = 1.0 + Z * e_charge**2 / (a * k_boltz * T)\n            else:\n                S_e = max(1.0 + Z * e_charge**2 / (a * k_boltz * T), 0.0)\n            \n            k_total += v_e * sigma * S_e * pop.dn_da[i] * pop.da[i]\n        \n        return k_total\n    \n    # --------------------------------------------------------\n    # Summary / diagnostics\n    # --------------------------------------------------------\n    \n    def summary(self):\n        \"\"\"Print a summary of the dust model state.\"\"\"\n        print(f\"THEMIS Dust Model:\")\n        print(f\"  Nano-grain fraction: {self.f_nano:.3f} (1.0 = diffuse ISM)\")\n        print(f\"  Band gap E_g: {self.E_g:.2f} eV\")\n        print(f\"  \u03c3_FUV per H: {self.sigma_FUV_per_H():.2e} cm\u00b2\")\n        print(f\"  A_V/N_H: {self.A_V_per_NH():.2e} mag cm\u00b2\")\n        for pop in self.populations:\n            print(f\"  {pop.name}: n/H = {pop.total_number():.2e}, \"\n                  f\"area/H = {pop.total_surface_area():.2e} cm\u00b2/H, \"\n                  f\"mass/H = {pop.mass_per_H():.2e} g/H\")\n        print(f\"  R_H2 (T=50K, Td=20K): {self.h2_formation_rate(50, 20):.2e} cm\u00b3/s\")\n",

      "code": "cd /home/claude && timeout 30 python3 -c \"\nimport sys; sys.path.insert(0, '/home/claude')\nfor m in list(sys.modules.keys()):\n    if 'prism3d' in m: del sys.modules[m]\n\nfrom prism3d.grains.themis import THEMISDust\nimport numpy as np\n\nprint('='*65)\nprint('  THEMIS Dust Model Validation')\nprint('='*65)\n\n# Standard diffuse ISM\ndust = THEMISDust(f_nano=1.0, E_g=0.1)\ndust.summary()\n\nprint()\nprint('--- PE Heating Comparison ---')\n# Compare with BT94 at standard conditions\nfrom prism3d.thermal.heating import photoelectric_heating as pe_bt94\n\nfor G0, n_H, T in [(1, 100, 100), (10, 1000, 50), (1e4, 1e5, 300)]:\n    x_e = 1.4e-4  # C+ dominated\n    Gamma_bt94 = pe_bt94(n_H, T, G0, x_e)\n    Gamma_themis = dust.photoelectric_heating(n_H, T, G0, x_e)\n    ratio = Gamma_themis / Gamma_bt94 if Gamma_bt94 > 0 else 0\n    print(f'  G0={G0:.0e}, n={n_H:.0e}, T={T}: '\n          f'BT94={Gamma_bt94:.2e}  THEMIS={Gamma_themis:.2e}  ratio={ratio:.2f}')\n\nprint()\nprint('--- H2 Formation Rate ---')\nfor T_gas, T_dust in [(50, 15), (100, 25), (300, 50), (1000, 100)]:\n    R = dust.h2_formation_rate(T_gas, T_dust)\n    print(f'  T_gas={T_gas:4d}K, T_dust={T_dust:3d}K: R_H2 = {R:.2e} cm\u00b3/s'\n          f'  (standard: 3e-17)')\n\nprint()\nprint('--- Dust Temperature ---')\nfor G0 in [1, 10, 100, 1e4]:\n    T_eq = dust.equilibrium_temperature(G0)\n    for name, T_arr in T_eq.items():\n        T_med = np.median(T_arr)\n        T_min = np.min(T_arr)\n        T_max = np.max(T_arr)\n        print(f'  G0={G0:6.0f}: {name:10s} T = {T_min:.0f}-{T_max:.0f} K (med {T_med:.0f})')\n\nprint()\nprint('--- Nano-grain Depletion (Orion Bar edge) ---')\ndust_orion = THEMISDust(f_nano=0.07, E_g=0.01)  # Elyajouri+ 2024\ndust_orion.summary()\nprint()\nGamma_std = dust.photoelectric_heating(1e5, 300, 2e4, 1e-4)\nGamma_dep = dust_orion.photoelectric_heating(1e5, 300, 2e4, 1e-4)\nprint(f'  PE heating standard:  {Gamma_std:.2e} erg/cm\u00b3/s')\nprint(f'  PE heating depleted:  {Gamma_dep:.2e} erg/cm\u00b3/s')\nprint(f'  Reduction factor:     {Gamma_dep/Gamma_std:.2f}')\n\nprint()\nprint('--- Dust Evolution ---')\n# Simulate exposure to strong FUV\ndust_evo = THEMISDust(f_nano=1.0, E_g=0.1)\nG0 = 1e4; n_H = 1e4\nfor t_kyr in [0.1, 1, 10, 100, 1000]:\n    dt = t_kyr * 1e3 * 3.15e7\n    f_new, Eg_new = dust_evo.evolve(G0, n_H, dt)\n    print(f'  t={t_kyr:6.1f} kyr: f_nano={f_new:.3f}, E_g={Eg_new:.3f} eV')\n    dust_evo.f_nano = f_new\n    dust_evo.E_g = Eg_new\n\" 2>&1",
